evals.prompt: I want to start a new side project called recipe-box. It's a recipe organizer app. Set up the project for me.
evals.expected_output: Uses the scaffold script, correct conventions, defers Firebase

assertions.text: Uses new-project.sh scaffold script instead of manual ng new
Project name is recipe-box
Does not install Tailwind, Sass, Angular Material, or @angular/fire
Defers Firebase setup — does not create Firebase project, install SDK, or set up DNS unless the user explicitly asked for it

evals.prompt: I need a bookmarks feature for my app. Users can save URLs with a title and tags, and see them in a list. Add the feature with the component, routing, and a Firestore service for persistence.
evals.expected_output: Creates bookmarks/ at top level with 2025 naming, OnPush, signals, Firebase JS SDK, security rules

assertions.text: Feature folder is bookmarks/ at top level (not nested under features/ or pages/)
Uses 2025 naming convention (bookmarks.ts not bookmarks.component.ts, no Component/Service suffixes)
All components use ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush
Uses signals for state (signal/computed/effect, not BehaviorSubject/RxJS)
Uses Firebase JS SDK directly (not @angular/fire)
Includes Firestore security rules with auth-owns-data pattern
Firestore service injects from core/ wrapper (e.g. core/firebase), not raw Firebase imports
Uses DestroyRef to clean up onSnapshot listener if service is not providedIn root, or if component manages the listener lifecycle

evals.prompt: My project mood-tracker is ready to go live. Set up Firebase App Hosting, configure the subdomain mood-tracker.charlies.bot, and deploy it.
evals.expected_output: Uses App Hosting, apphosting.yaml, gcloud MCP for DNS, charlies-bot zone

assertions.text: Uses Firebase App Hosting (not classic firebase deploy --only hosting)
Creates or references apphosting.yaml with appropriate config
Uses run_gcloud_command MCP tool for DNS setup
DNS records target charlies-bot zone specifically
Uses firebase apphosting:backends:create CLI command for backend setup (Firebase MCP does not support App Hosting)
Warns user that the backends:create command will open their browser for GitHub connection via Developer Connect
Deploys via git push (not firebase deploy for hosting)

evals.prompt: I need an API endpoint in my app that takes a URL and returns the page title and meta description. Add a server route for it.
evals.expected_output: Uses Angular server routes on App Hosting, not Cloud Functions

assertions.text: Creates an Angular server route (not a Cloud Function)
Does not suggest firebase deploy --only functions for this endpoint
The endpoint runs on the same App Hosting Node.js server as the Angular app
Does not create a separate functions/ directory or firebase-functions dependency for this
